seo.title: Security — Qarote RabbitMQ Monitoring
seo.description: How Qarote protects your RabbitMQ credentials, handles your data, and keeps your monitoring infrastructure safe. Self-hosted option means your data never leaves your network.

hero.title: Built for teams who can't afford a breach.
hero.subtitle: You're connecting Qarote to your message broker. Here's exactly what we do with that access — and what we don't.

items.title: Credentials encrypted at rest
items.description: RabbitMQ connection credentials are encrypted with AES-256 before being written to disk. Your passwords are never stored in plaintext.

items.title: TLS 1.2+ in transit
items.description: All traffic between your browser and Qarote's API is encrypted with TLS 1.2 or higher. Connections to your broker over TLS are also supported and recommended.

items.title: Message payloads never leave your broker
items.description: Qarote reads queue depths, message rates, and consumer counts to power alerting and trend charts — retaining those metrics for up to 7 days. The actual contents of your messages are never accessed, read, or stored.

items.title: Workspace isolation
items.description: Each workspace is fully isolated. Users can only access brokers and data within their workspace. Cross-tenant data access is architecturally impossible.

items.title: Role-based access control
items.description: Assign admin or read-only roles per workspace member. Limit who can modify connections, configure alerts, or access sensitive broker details.

items.title: Session management
items.description: Sessions are short-lived and invalidated on logout. We support SSO via OIDC and SAML for enterprise teams with centralised identity management.

selfHosted.heading: Your network. Your rules.
selfHosted.body: Qarote can be deployed entirely on your own infrastructure — bare metal, Docker, or Kubernetes. In self-hosted mode, no telemetry, no credentials, and no monitoring data ever leave your network. You get the full product with zero dependency on Qarote's cloud.

disclosure.heading: Found a vulnerability?
disclosure.body: We take security reports seriously. If you discover a vulnerability in Qarote, please email us at security@qarote.io. We'll acknowledge your report within 48 hours and keep you updated as we work on a fix. We do not pursue legal action against good-faith security researchers.

items.question: Do you store my RabbitMQ password?
items.answer: Yes — but only if you use the cloud edition and need Qarote to poll your broker on your behalf. Passwords are encrypted with AES-256 at rest. In self-hosted mode, credentials stay on your own server.

items.question: Does Qarote read my message payloads?
items.answer: No. Qarote accesses the RabbitMQ management API to read queue depths, message rates, and node health. It does not consume or inspect message payloads.

items.question: Is my broker exposed to the internet to use Qarote?
items.answer: No. You can run the Qarote agent inside your private network. It connects outbound to Qarote's API — your broker management port never needs to be publicly accessible.

items.question: Do you have SOC 2 certification?
items.answer: We are working toward SOC 2 Type II. In the meantime, our self-hosted edition is the right choice for teams with strict compliance requirements — you control the entire stack.

items.question: Can I use my own SSO provider?
items.answer: Yes. The Enterprise edition supports OIDC and SAML-based SSO, allowing you to plug Qarote into your existing identity provider (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace, etc.).
